page.content: The increasing interest in home gardening has gained significant attention in recent years, particularly as individuals seek sustainable practices that enhance food security and promote well-being. The data presented in the accompanying table provides insights into the availability of home gardening among the surveyed population. Notably, the "Frequency" column indicates that 123 respondents reported having access to home gardening options, highlighting a growing trend towards self-sufficiency in food production.

page.content: This constitutes 16.0 percent of the total respondents, as reflected in the "Percent" column, suggesting that while a substantial minority engages in this practice, there remains considerable potential for growth in home gardening initiatives. Factors contributing to this interest may include rising food costs, an increased awareness of environmental sustainability, and the mental health benefits associated with engaging in gardening activities. Understanding the frequency and prevalence of home gardening can inform policymakers and community organizations as they develop programs and resources to foster this practice. By leveraging the insights garnered from this data, stakeholders can better address the needs and preferences of the population, ultimately encouraging a culture of gardening that aligns with contemporary sustainability goals.
